incorrects.description: Student selected 2 × 3: reversed the groups and items per group

params.description: The 3 bags highlight sequentially to emphasize the number of groups.

steps.text: Count the bags first. That's your groups. How many bags do you see?

incorrects.description: Student selected 3 + 2: used addition instead of multiplication

params.description: Bags highlight to show the equal groups structure.

steps.text: When you have equal groups, you multiply. The × symbol shows groups of the same size. How many groups? How many in each?

incorrects.description: Student selected 2 + 3: used addition and reversed the numbers

params.description: Bags highlight to show the equal groups structure.

steps.text: For equal groups, use multiplication. Count the bags. That's the first number. Count inside one bag. That's the second number.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Bags highlight sequentially as they are counted: 1, 2, 3.

steps.text: Let me show you. First, I count the bags: 1, 2, 3. Three bags. That's the groups. Next, I look inside one bag: 1, 2. Two items in each. When I have 3 groups of 2, I write 3 × 2. The first number is the groups, the second is how many in each group.
